description_long: This is a rectangular wooden filing cabinet with a natural wood grain finish, featuring visible knots and patterns typical of pine or similar softwood. The cabinet has a simple, boxy design with clean edges and a flat top, suitable for stacking or placing items on top. On at least two sides, there are recessed horizontal slots that serve as handles for opening the drawers, blending subtly into the wood surface. The cabinet appears to be a single-drawer or multi-drawer unit, but only one handle is visible per side, suggesting a minimalist design. There are no visible label holders or external locks, giving it a streamlined and modern appearance. The overall construction suggests it is intended for indoor use, likely in an office, home, or archive setting.
description: A wooden filing cabinet with a natural grain finish, featuring recessed handles and a minimalist, boxy design. No visible label holders or locks are present.
description_view_0: Front view showing a recessed horizontal handle centered on the wooden face, with visible wood grain and knots.
description_view_1: Side view with no handle, displaying uninterrupted wood grain and a prominent knot in the center.
description_view_2: Rear or alternate front view with a similar recessed handle as view 0, but with a slightly different wood grain pattern.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with no handle, showing a smoother wood grain and subtle knots.
